vault.heading: Access your private planning
vault.subtitle: Your tasks stay local, private, and encrypted.
vault.willCreate: A new vault will be created at this location.
vault.instruction: Choose an existing vault or create a new one to begin.
vault.passwordRequired: Enter a password to create the vault.

overdue: Pending tasks whose due time has passed.
week: Tasks due in the next few days.
noDate: Open tasks without a due date.

overdue.title: Overdue
overdue.subtitle: Pending tasks whose due time has passed.

reminders.title: Reminders
reminders.subtitle: Pending tasks with a notification configured.

overdue: Reminders that have passed and still need attention.
week: Reminders scheduled for the next few days.

archived.title: Archived
archived.subtitle: Completed tasks preserved outside your working views.
archived.emptyMessage: Tasks archived automatically or manually will appear here.

task.title: Title
task.titlePlaceholder: What needs to be done? Use + to add tags

search.title: Search
search.subtitle: Find tasks by title or note content.
search.promptTitle: What do you need to find?
search.prompt: Type a word or use filters to search your vault.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Local preferences and secure Praxis status.
settings.languageDescription: Choose the language used throughout the application.
settings.appearanceDescription: Choose matte paper for bright rooms or inverted ink for low light.
settings.notificationsDescription: Allow pending task reminders to display notifications.
settings.badge: Show badge on the app icon
settings.badgeDescription: Display the pending task count on the application icon when available.
settings.startWindowsDescription: Open Praxis automatically when Windows starts.
settings.startMinimizedDescription: Keep Praxis discreet when it starts automatically.
settings.minimizeTrayDescription: Closing the unlocked window keeps Praxis running in the background.
settings.updatesDescription: Download signed releases published on GitHub.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Create reusable contexts. Name and color changes appear immediately on related tasks.
tags.empty: No tags yet. Praxis can also create a tag when you type + in a task title.

help.title: How to use Praxis
help.intro: A concise guide to capture what matters, receive the right reminder, and finish the day without forgotten tasks.
help.shortcutFree: Create a task without a preset context

access.title: Open your vault
access.description: The .praxis file is the source of your data. It stays local and encrypted, including when stored in a folder synchronized by you.
steps: Select an existing .praxis file or choose where to create a new vault.
Enter the password. Content opens only after the correct password is validated.
Use Lock to close the vault. While it is unlocked, closing the window keeps reminders and the badge active.

capture.title: Create without losing context
capture.description: Praxis supports contextual and context-free creation, so you can capture quickly while retaining control over dates, reminders, notes, tags, and checklists.
capture.contextLabel: Uses the current screen and selected day as context.
capture.contextBody: In My Day, the task is due today. In My Week, it receives the day selected in the calendar.
capture.freeLabel: Opens a task without a preset date.
capture.freeBody: Use it when you want to define every detail manually or simply capture an open task without a deadline.

views.title: Use each view to answer one question
views.description: Views are not folders. Each one answers a practical question and loads only the tasks it needs.
views.today: What is overdue, due today, or completed today.
views.week: The next seven days, starting tomorrow.
views.pending: Everything still open, grouped by urgency.
views.reminders: Only pending tasks with a notification configured.

reminder.title: Due dates and reminders serve different purposes
reminder.description: A due date defines urgency and lateness. A reminder defines when Windows should call your attention.
steps: Set a due date to indicate when the task must be ready.
Set a reminder only when you want a notification at a specific time.
Keep notifications enabled in Praxis settings and in Windows.
reminder.exampleNote: Due at 4:00 PM with a reminder thirty minutes earlier.

details.title: Break work down without creating new obligations
details.description: Checklists and tags enrich a task, but only the parent task has a due date, reminder, recurrence, and badge participation.
details.checklist: Use a checklist for visual steps. When every item is completed, the parent task is completed automatically.
details.tags: Use tags to identify contexts such as work, personal, or finance. Renaming or recoloring a tag updates every related task.
details.inlineTag: When creating or editing a task, type + in the title to search for a tag. If it does not exist, confirm with Enter to create and assign it automatically.

lifecycle.title: Complete, reopen, or restore
lifecycle.description: Praxis preserves the task lifecycle so its history remains useful without occupying working views.
lifecycle.toggle: Click the task indicator to switch between pending and completed.
lifecycle.archive: Older completed tasks can be archived automatically according to the policy selected in Settings.
lifecycle.restore: In Archived, use the blue restore icon beside the trash button. The original completion date is preserved.

privacy.title: Adjust the app without exposing your vault
privacy.description: Settings includes appearance, notifications, badge, startup, retention, and safe technical indicators.
privacy.database: Database status shows whether the vault is open, when the file was updated, and whether encryption is active, without revealing the password or content.
privacy.theme: Choose between light and dark E-Ink themes. Priority colors remain stable in both modes.

help.supportTitle: Built with care, supported by people who believe in it
help.supportDescription: Meet the person behind Praxis and, if it makes sense to you, help keep the project moving forward.
help.supportVisualBody: Support funds development and maintenance, not barriers for people who simply need to organize their day.
help.supportVisualNote: Local privacy, reliable reminders, and a calm experience remain at the center of the product.
help.footerTitle: Ready to get back to work
help.footerBody: The essentials fit into creating, remembering, and completing.

support.intro1: Praxis is independently created and maintained by {name}, with special attention to privacy, performance, and the details that make daily use calmer.
support.intro2: If Praxis helps you remember what matters, a voluntary contribution supports development, Windows testing, and the project’s continuity. Praxis will continue to work normally without a donation.
support.internationalDescription: Support Praxis through Buy Me a Coffee in your system browser.
support.openError: The browser could not be opened.
support.pixDescription: Any amount helps keep development active.
support.copyError: Windows did not allow the key to be copied. Try again.
support.pixHint: Scan the QR code or copy the key. You choose the amount.
support.qrAlt: Pix QR code to support Praxis development

timeline.label: Task timeline
timeline.empty: No events recorded for this task.
timeline.name: Name
